claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded throughput snapshots for Llama 3.2 1B Instruct Q8_0 and Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB), 601-token prefill + short-context 128-token greedy decode, three same-session rounds with alternating runtime order per row. 1B: Camelid decode reads above both comparators in every round (median 74.79 vs llama.cpp 67.20 and MLX-LM 69.66); 1B prefill reads above llama.cpp (1664.3 vs 1472.8) and parity-level with MLX-LM (1670.0, the medians differ by less than inter-round spread — no win claimed against MLX on this lane). 8B: Camelid prefill reads narrowly above both (234.2 vs 220.4 / 229.2); 8B decode is a three-way parity band (12.09 / 12.14 / 12.04 — no win claimed, the lane is weight-bandwidth-bound). Same-session snapshots on exact rows; nothing transfers.

workload.rows: Llama 3.2 1B Instruct Q8_0 (bartowski GGUF; mlx-community 1B 8bit) and Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 (QuantFactory GGUF; mlx-community 8B 8bit)
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate two-point decode + 601-token verbose prompt pass

parity: Camelid greedy continuations identical across every iteration of every round on both rows (output_token_ids in the jsonl logs).
